content.question:
Put the verbs in brackets into an appropriate past tense.

1. He walked (walk) to the front of the stage, ___ (take) a bow and ___ (wave) to the audience.

Put the verbs in brackets into an appropriate past tense.

2. We ___ (not/enjoy) the play so we ___ (leave) early.

Put the verbs in brackets into an appropriate past tense.

3. While the patients ___ (wait) in the surgery, the doctor ___ (deal) with a complicated case.

Put the verbs in brackets into an appropriate past tense.

4. I'm starving - I ___ (have) breakfast over five hours ago.

Put the verbs in brackets into an appropriate past tense.

5. In June 1979 they ___ (still / build) this shopping centre.

Put the verbs in brackets into an appropriate past tense.

6. They ___ (walk) to the water's edge, ___ (wade) in and ___ (swim) to the other side.

Put the verbs in brackets into an appropriate past tense.

7. While the soldiers ___ (advance) they did not realise that the enemy ___ (plan) a surprise attack.
content.answer: were advancing ; was planning / were planning

content.question:
Put the verbs in brackets into an appropriate past tense.

8. She ___ (set) out for an appointment when the lights ___ (go off).

Put the verbs in brackets into an appropriate past tense.

9. By the time the police ___ (locate) the stolen car, the thieves ___ (already / escape) to France.

Put the verbs in brackets into an appropriate past tense.

10. Her clothes were wet because she ___ (walk) in the rain.
